calendar.matters:
Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: E. MICHAEL LINSCHEID, ESQ., 415.782.6002, FOR PETITIONER GERARD MCMENAMY.
AUSTIN J. CATTERMOLE, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 510.622.2190, FOR RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Petitioner Gerary McMenamy's Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate is denied. The exclusionary rule does not apply to the DMV proceeding under the facts of this case. See Park v. Valverde (2007) 152 Cal. App. 4th 877. The officer had reasonable suspicion to stop petitioner's vehicle when he stopped six feet before the crosswalk, drove through the stop sign intersection at about 25 mph without stopping, continued driving although the officer had turned on his car red lights and siren, and then drove inbound into the outbound traffic lane before ultimately stopping in the road. Petitioner's premature stop 6 feet before the crosswalk was sufficient to warrant the officer's investigative stop. The officer's subsequent investigation, including the objective signs of intoxication, resulted in probable cause to believe petitioner was driving under the influence of alcohol. Petitioner was lawfully arrested. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Hearing And Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER JONATHAN GERARD MCMENAMY'S Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate is continued to May 2, 2013 on the Court's motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm
